1.raw:
# Stage 1: QiMeng Yu ShiCi

## Description
From zero to understanding shortest sentences.

## Grammar
1. 48 IPA symbols
2. Noun singular/plural
3. Personal pronouns
4. be verbs (am/is/are)

## Vocabulary
About 500 core words

## Milestone
Understand simple sentences like I am a student.
1.title: Stage 1: QiMeng Yu ShiCi

2.raw:
## Stage 2: Sentences
Ability: read basic sentences, understand S+V+O structure
Grammar: 5 basic sentence patterns, 4 tenses
Vocabulary: 1000-1500 words
Milestone: 4/5 correct on reading detail questions

Grammar details:
1. Five patterns: S+V, S+V+O, S+V+C, S+V+IO+DO, S+V+O+C
2. Simple present: I go / She goes
3. Present progressive: I am going
4. Simple past: I went (regular: -ed; irregular: go-went, do-did, have-had, see-saw, eat-ate)
5. Simple future: will go / am going to go
grammar: 1. Five patterns: S+V, S+V+O, S+V+C, S+V+IO+DO, S+V+O+C
2. Simple present: I go / She goes
4. Simple past: I went (regular: -ed; irregular: go-went, do-did, have-had, see-saw, eat-ate)
5. Simple future: will go / am going to go

3.raw:
## Stage 3: Grammar Framework
Ability: Identify clauses, passive voice, perfect tense
Grammar: Perfect tenses, passive voice, 3 clauses, non-finite verbs
Vocabulary: 2000-2500 words
Milestone: 60%+ on grammar single-choice

Grammar details:
1. Present perfect: have/has + done (already, yet, ever, never, since, for)
2. Past perfect: had + done
3. Passive voice: be + done (by...)
4. Attributive clause: who(人)/which(物)/that(人/物)/whose(所属)
5. Noun clause: that/what/whether
6. Adverbial clause: when/because/if/although
7. Non-finite: gerund(doing), infinitive(to do)
grammar: 1. Present perfect: have/has + done (already, yet, ever, never, since, for)
4. Attributive clause: who(人)/which(物)/that(人/物)/whose(所属)

4.raw:
## Stage 4: Exam Skills
Ability: Convert knowledge to scores with techniques
Key techniques:
1. Reading: Read Q stem -> Locate original text -> Find synonym replacement
2. Cloze: Use context logic (contrast/cause-effect) + collocations
3. Writing: Master templates (letter + argumentative essay)
Vocabulary: 3000+ words
Milestone: 60%+ on full practice test (no writing)
Key collocations: look forward to, be responsible for, pay attention to, take part in, have access to
grammar: 1. Reading: Read Q stem -> Locate original text -> Find synonym replacement

5.raw:
## Stage 5: Final Sprint
Ability: Stable exam performance
Key areas:
1. Mock tests: Strict timing
2. Writing templates: 1-3 personal templates
3. Mistake review: Repeat error book
4. Word meanings: book(v), run(v), stand(v), charge(v), leave(v)
Vocabulary: 3400-4000 words
Milestone: 65+ on mock tests
5. Exam order: Writing first -> Vocab/grammar -> Reading -> Cloze
grammar: 4. Word meanings: book(v), run(v), stand(v), charge(v), leave(v)
5. Exam order: Writing first -> Vocab/grammar -> Reading -> Cloze

references.writing_templates:
# Writing Templates

## Letter (Letter)
Dear ___,
I am writing to you to ___.
First of all, ___. In addition, ___. Last but not least, ___.
I am looking forward to your reply.
Yours sincerely,
[Name]

## Argumentative Essay
As we all know, ___ has become a hot topic.
Different people have different opinions. Some think ___. Others argue ___.
As far as I am concerned, ___. Only in this way can we ___.
In conclusion, it is important to ___.

## Opening sentences
- I am writing to express my...
- It is well-known that...
- With the development of...
- Recently, the issue of... has aroused wide concern.

## Transition words
- First of all / To begin with
- In addition / Moreover / Furthermore
- However / On the other hand
- Therefore / As a result
- Last but not least
- In conclusion / To sum up
references.common_mistakes:
# Chinese Learners' Top 10 Mistakes
1. Tense mixing: I go to school yesterday -> I went to school yesterday
2. Missing -s for 3rd person: He go to school -> He goes to school
3. Missing article: I have book -> I have a book
4. There be vs have: There has a book -> There is a book
5. Because + so: Because I was tired, so I slept -> Because I was tired, I slept
6. Subject-verb disagreement: The students is happy -> The students are happy
7. Wrong preposition: Depend in -> Depend on; Discuss about -> Discuss
8. Adj/adv confusion: runs quick -> runs quickly
9. Wrong relative pronoun: The book who I bought -> The book which/that I bought
10. Non-finite verb mixing: enjoy to read -> enjoy reading
